chapter_title.en: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them

text: 702 - وَقَالَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «صَلاَةٌ فِي بَيْتِ اَلْمَقْدِسِ تَعْدِلُ أَلْفَ صَلاَةٍ وَ صَلاَةٌ فِي اَلْمَسْجِدِ اَلْأَعْظَمِ تَعْدِلُ مِائَةَ أَلْفِ صَلاَةٍ وَ صَلاَةٌ فِي مَسْجِدِ اَلْقَبِيلَةِ تَعْدِلُ خَمْساً وَ عِشْرِينَ صَلاَةً وَ صَلاَةٌ فِي مَسْجِدِ اَلسُّوقِ، تَعْدِلُ اِثْنَتَيْ عَشْرَةَ صَلاَةً وَ صَلاَةُ اَلرَّجُلِ فِي بَيْتِهِ تَعْدِلُ صَلاَةً وَاحِدَةً.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.702 - Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said :
"A prayer in Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem) is equivalent to one thousand prayers. A prayer in the Great Mosque (Masjid al-Haram) is equivalent to one hundred thousand prayers. A prayer in the Mosque of the Tribe (Masjid al-Qabilah) is equivalent to twenty-five prayers. A prayer in the market mosque is equivalent to twelve prayers. A man's prayer in his home is equivalent to one prayer."
